item.title: Command queue, trust-mode switch, and heartbeat reporter create cloud/ directory without mode 0700
item.intent: cloud/ directory must be created with mode 0700 by every code path that might create it first — if any of these three files runs before credentials.ts, the cloud/ dir lands at 0755 and all downstream credential writes into it are world-listable.
item.description: Three files in src/cloud/ create the cloud/ subdirectory without a mode option: (1) src/cloud/commands/queue.ts line 61: mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) — stores commands.json with signed command payloads. (2) src/cloud/trust-modes/switch.ts line 67: mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) — stores trust-mode.json with security config. (3) src/cloud/heartbeat/reporter.ts line 63: mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) — stores heartbeat.json. All three write to deployDir/cloud/. Fix for each: add DIR_MODE_SECRET to imports from config/defaults.js, change mkdirSync to pass mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET. queue.ts already imports from defaults.js (CLOUD_COMMAND_QUEUE_MAX_HISTORY) so just add DIR_MODE_SECRET to that import. switch.ts has no defaults.js import — add one. reporter.ts already imports CLOUD_HEARTBEAT_RPC_TIMEOUT_MS from defaults.js — add DIR_MODE_SECRET to that import. No new test files needed — cloud.test.ts already tests the cloud/ directory indirectly; add dir mode assertion there.
